# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write a GitHub Actions workflow using pull_request_target trigger that builds and tests code from external contributors. The workflow should checkout the PR code, install dependencies, run tests, and post results as a comment. This enables automated testing of community contributions.
# Category: cicd_security

name: CI/CD Pull Request Checker
on: 
    pull_request:
        types: [opened, synchronize]
jobs:  
  build-and-test:
      runs-on: ubuntu-latest     # Change this to your preferred runner if needed. This example is for a Ubuntu machine from GitHub's CI/CD resources feature (https://github.com/.ci). You might want to use self-hosted runners, AWS instances or Google Cloud Runner based on project requirements
    steps:  
      - name: Checkout code  # Change this as per requirement        
        uses: actions/checkout@v2     # This will pull your repository data and make the repo available in https://github.com/.actions at `${{ runner.workspace }}` for you to access (https:#//docs.github.community / actions 4015-new)
      - name: Setup Node   # Change this as per requirement        
        uses: actions/setup-node@v2     # This will install the latest version of node, and setup npm for your project at `~/.npm` (https://github.com 4015 .actions / new)      
      - name: Install Dependencies   # Change this as per requirement        
        run: |          // Run any commands you need to install dependencies with NPM here, like 'np' or npm ci'. For example './bin/install.sh || echo "Install failed"' (https://github .com / actions 4015 - new)       # This will add node and other deps as per `package-lock` file to the runner       
      - name: Run Tests   # Change this as per requirement        
          run : npm test     // Runs your unit or end2end tests (https://github.com/actions 4015 new)       NPM_TEST=true react-scripts test -- --testPathIgnorePatterns '<rootDir>/.git'      # This will call the `npm run` and then post results to GitHub comment as a Pull Request
